privateProjectInfo.title: Project protected by licensing and/or confidentiality agreements.
privateProjectInfo.description: Due to legal reasons, such as the LGPD (General Data Protection Law) and corporate privacy policies, the project view links and repositories are not available. The source code is proprietary and protected by specific licenses, ensuring the security and integrity of the information involved.

dkpedras.title: Freelance Project: DK Pedras
dkpedras.description: Freelance project: Development of a website for a store with a product catalog. Responsible for prototyping, design, implementation of features, and maintenance. I optimized image latency using Cloudinary with CDN for efficient loading, maximizing the resources of the free plan.

nutrien.title: Digital Platform Nutrien
nutrien.description: Development of a management and retail platform for agricultural inputs, with an architecture based on microservices and micro frontends, in addition to an API Gateway. Responsible for task refinement, implementation of new features, bug fixes, integration with Microsoft Teams, processing of pricing spreadsheets in Excel and PDFs, and developing order negotiation flows, product catalog, and shopping cart.

sms.title: Soil Monitoring System
sms.description: A project focused on controlling and taking care of domestic plants, using NPK, temperature, pH, light sensors, and image capture to evaluate the health of plants. It includes a modern digital platform for data analysis, charts, health reports, record history, PDF export, dark mode, and an admin panel for species parameterization, simulation mode, and other features.

timekeeping.title: Timekeeping System
timekeeping.description: Internal system for tracking worked hours.

hospital.title: Data Transfer Between Healthcare Systems
hospital.description: Development of integration between the medical systems Tasy and PEP, correlating events and data in a unified database. Implementation of a hexagonal architecture to optimize performance and communication between applications, along with the creation of unit tests to ensure system reliability.

projectExperience.title: Participation in Over 5 Successful Projects
projectExperience.description: Significant contributions to various high-complexity projects, both professional and academic, always focused on quality and results.

technologyExpert.title: Expert in Cutting-Edge Technologies
technologyExpert.description: Expertise in React, TypeScript, Node.js, NestJS, PostgreSQL, MongoDB, among other high-performance tools and libraries, to create robust and scalable solutions.

interpersonalSkills.title: Interpersonal Skills
interpersonalSkills.description: Advanced communication skills, with a focus on knowledge transfer, as well as persistence in solving complex problems. Ability to organize processes and promote a collaborative and productive work environment.

clientExperience.title: Client and Business Experience
clientExperience.description: Experience in various projects, including healthcare systems, time tracking, product catalogs, and the largest agricultural digital sales platform in Brazil.

agileDevelopment.title: Agile Development and Efficiency
agileDevelopment.description: Experience with agile methodologies such as Scrum and Kanban, optimizing the development process with a focus on quick and high-quality deliveries, including task refinement and timeline estimation.

softwareArchitecture.title: Software Architecture and Best Practices
softwareArchitecture.description: Implementation of Clean Architecture, Clean Code, and scalable design practices, such as modular and hexagonal architecture, ensuring the longevity and maintainability of the code.

problem.title: Your clinic may be invisible to people ready to book
problem.subtitle: Without a strategic page, patients with real booking intent often find your competitors first on Google.
problem.item2: ❌ Low visibility in local city searches
problem.item3: ❌ No clear WhatsApp booking call to action
problem.item4: ❌ Overdependence on referrals and social media
problem.item5: ❌ Your outreach spend does not turn into a fuller schedule
problem.item6: ❌ You cannot clearly measure what brings contacts versus only visits
problem.sideTitle: The core issue is usually not your clinical quality. It is the lack of a digital structure to capture patients who are ready to book.
problem.sidePoint1: High-intent patients usually choose the clinic that appears first on Google.
problem.sidePoint2: Without a strategic landing page, lead costs tend to rise while conversion drops.
problem.sidePoint3: With local positioning and the right message, your schedule becomes more predictable.
problem.footer: With a simple strategic structure, your clinic turns searches into contacts and contacts into bookings.

solution.title: A professional website that positions your clinic to grow
solution.subtitle: You do not just need a good-looking website. You need a structure that builds trust, highlights your clinic, and attracts more ready-to-book patients.
solution.card1Text: Design and messaging tailored to your clinic profile to create stronger credibility from first contact.
solution.card2Title: Digital presence that drives local discovery
solution.card2Text: Structure prepared for your clinic to be found by people searching for a dentist in your city.
solution.card3Text: Fast, organized, and easy-to-navigate pages that reinforce quality perception and patient confidence.
solution.card4Text: Calls to action and WhatsApp triggers in the right places to turn visits into commercial conversations.
solution.footer: When your clinic combines professional presentation with conversion strategy, your schedule stops relying only on referrals.
solution.cta: I want this structure for my clinic

demo.title: Strategic example: Gabriela Molina Clinic
demo.subtitle: This section was crafted from the clinic's real positioning to show how credibility, clear offer framing, and lead generation work together.
demo.strategyTitle: How this demo was structured to convert
demo.item1: An evaluation offer is highlighted in the first fold to lower entry friction and generate conversations faster.
demo.item2: Dr. Gabriela's presence is used as a trust element to increase confidence before first contact.
demo.item3: WhatsApp calls to action are placed at decision points to shorten the path to booking.
demo.item4: The structure speaks to non-technical patients and answers common concerns without technical language.
demo.siteImageAlt: Preview of Gabriela Molina Clinic website
demo.ctaNote: This reference shows the level of structure and finish that can be applied to your clinic landing page.

differentials.title: Differentials that turn digital presence into a fuller schedule
differentials.subtitle: Each decision in structure, messaging, and positioning is designed to increase qualified leads and reduce patient acquisition cost.
differentials.card1Title: Positioning for clinic owners, not for technical audiences
differentials.card1Text: The communication speaks to decision-makers who care about return: more appointments, better attendance rates, and predictable growth.
differentials.card2Text: We define offer, procedure priorities, and value argument first. Then design is used to amplify conversion.
differentials.card3Title: Messaging that lowers objections and builds trust
differentials.card3Text: Conversion copy is built to answer common patient concerns and speed up the decision to contact your clinic.
differentials.card4Text: A direct journey for fast action: clear offer, credibility proof, and strategic calls to action across the page.
differentials.card5Title: Local SEO to capture ready-to-book demand
differentials.card5Text: The page is structured for city-level searches with real appointment intent, attracting warmer traffic.
differentials.card6Text: You get solid technical implementation with a page structure designed to convert better, without relying on rigid template solutions.

process.title: How the process works
process.subtitle: Simple, direct, and frictionless.
process.step1Text: We align your priority treatments, city, and business goals.
process.step2Text: We build the structure, design, local SEO, and conversion-focused flow.
process.step3Text: Final adjustments, publishing, and WhatsApp/contact setup.
process.step4Text: Content updates and lightweight improvements to keep performance high.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.q1: How long does the landing page take to be ready?
faq.a1: Typical delivery is 7 to 14 days, depending on project complexity and how fast clinic information is provided.
faq.q2: Can this landing page help my clinic appear on Google?
faq.a2: Yes. The structure is optimized for local SEO, increasing your chances of appearing in searches like "dentist in [city]".
faq.q3: Do I need to already have a domain and hosting?
faq.a3: If you already have them, we use your current setup. If not, I guide you on the best options and setup.
faq.q4: What is included in monthly support?
faq.a4: Small content updates, information refreshes, and lightweight adjustments to keep the page updated.
